characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abusive Parents: His father was an abusive man who killed his mother in front of him. A young Caine killed him in an attempt to save her, but he was too late. Cartwright Curse: He has notoriously bad luck with girlfriends. They always seem to die. Character Tic: Turning his body to a side while looking at someone. Cool Shades: He owns an iconic pair of sunglasses. Many an episode starts with him dramatically putting the shades on and dropping a snappy one-liner about this week's victim. Cowboy Cop: Of the CSI leader bunch (Grissom and Taylor et al) Caine is the closest to this trope, with many cases ending with him pulling a*Click* HelloStealth Hi/Bye, shooting, beating up, or plain threatening suspects. David Caruso's gravel voice helps plenty.

characters.name: Horatio Caine
characters.description: Abusive Parents: His father was an abusive man who killed his mother in front of him. A young Caine killed him in an attempt to save her, but he was too late. Cartwright Curse: He has notoriously bad luck with girlfriends. They always seem to die. Character Tic: Turning his body to a side while looking at someone. Cool Shades: He owns an iconic pair of sunglasses. Many an episode starts with him dramatically putting the shades on and dropping a snappy one-liner about this week's victim. Cowboy Cop: Of the CSI leader bunch (Grissom and Taylor et al) Caine is the closest to this trope, with many cases ending with him pulling a*Click* HelloStealth Hi/Bye, shooting, beating up, or plain threatening suspects. David Caruso's gravel voice helps plenty.

characters.name: Calleigh Duquesne
characters.description: Ms. Fanservice: She's prone to wearing extremely tight pants and four-inch heels (due to Procter being the shortest cast member at 5'2), which almost always comes with the camera panning back toshow her legs. She is the only other cast member apart from Horatio to appear on UK DVD set covers, likely for this reason. Number Two: She takes over as Horatio's Assistant Supervisor after Megan's departure. Southern-Fried Genius: Blonde, gorgeous Southern belle - who knows more about ballistics than the rest of the team combined.

characters.name: Tim Speedle
characters.description: Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": Speed, very rarely ever Tim or Speedle. Fatal Flaw: He works for the police and has required to use his gun repeatedly for protection, but he never ever finds the time to clean the thing.Even after aConvenient Misfirealmost gets him killed in a shootout, he never learns his lesson.The second time he is not so lucky. Jerkass: Definitely has his moments, usually when speaking to women. He tells one victim of harassment that it was her fault, and that she'd led the guy on (she hadn't), and is rude to other female characters (excepting Alexx, Megan, Calleigh and Yelena). Killed Off for Real:Speed is shot and killed halfway through the Season 3 premiere by security guards at a jewelry store when his gun jams thanks to negligent keeping. Tempting Fate: When walking into a jewelry store with Horatio, Speed discusses expensive cars, disdaining them in favor of his bike. Caine mentions that he might need "something with doors" some day. Speed says he has plenty of time.He is fatally shot not five minutes later.

characters.name: Eric Delko
characters.description: The Mole:Was working for Internal Affairs during his relationship with Calleigh. Mysterious Parent:His biological father is a very dangerous Cuban gangster. Put on a Bus: To be onUgly Betty. Returned when it got cancelled. Revenge Before Reason: During one case, he and Tripp escort a suspect to the lab: he is a football rising star who woke up next to a dead girl and tried to flee. He admits it was wrong to run, but swears also he is innocent and that he did what he did because he panicked. As they approach the CSI office, many reporters are waiting outside the building, so he begs them not to expose him to the media so blatantly, as it would ruin his image and career for nothing. Eric and Tripp, however, do not believe his version, and the former declares that his attempt to escape is proof of his guilt, so from now on everything is on him, and he needs to know that.Too bad the boy eventually turns out to be innocent, but by then his football career is already in serious jeopardy thanks to what Eric and Tripp have done. And worst of all?Neither of them is shown to be remorseful for acting completely inappropriately(although it is suspicious, to run away from police is not proof of guilt) and ruining a boy's life, all because they were clouded by prejudice.

characters.name: Ryan Wolfe
characters.description: All Love Is Unrequited: Holds a massive torch for Boa Vista for most of the series, but she doesn't reciprocate his feelings. Eager Rookie: When he first joined the lab, he was intent on proving himself to Horatio as much as possible. This results in him having a tendency to be competitive against his co-workers, which rubs them off in the wrong way for a while. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Occasionally. He's usually called on it, though. Even so, he's proven to be a very capable cop, and extremely compassionate when he needs to be. Last-Name Basis: Horatio calls everyone else by their first names. Ryan, tho, is always "Mr. Wolfe." Yank the Dog's Chain: He seems to finally be building up a good relationship with newcomer Sam in the final season and is on track for aHappy Ending. Thenshe betrays him by leaving him behind to take the blame for killing her boyfriend in the very last episode.

characters.name: Natalia Boa Vista
characters.description: The Mole: When first introduced, she is actually a Mole for the FBI, reporting on the Labs activities while posing as an assistant Genetic Analyst. After getting to know and befriend all of theCSIsand seeing them all as the good cops and even better people that they are, reveals her status to Horatio, and sides with her friends, quitting her job at the FBI, and becoming a CSI herself.

characters.name: Maxine Valera
characters.description: The Lab Rat: The team's DNA analyst from season 2 to 8.

characters.name: Alexx Woods
characters.description: The Coroner: She's the team's Medical Examiner season 1 to 6. My Nayme Is: Spelled with one too many x's. Put on a Bus: Alexx leaves the morgue in Season 6 to become an ER doctor. She's occasionally appeared since then, usually when one of the team have been injured in the line of duty. She returns for a few appearances past that. Team Mom: Mostly to the unfortunate victims who arrive in the morgue, but she's also the most compassionate and sympathetic team member, always willing to lend an ear/support her colleagues.

characters.name: Tara Price
characters.description: The Coroner: She's the team's Medical Examiner in season 7. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: She replaces Alexx Woods asThe Coronerof the team in season 7, and is very similar in appearance to her. Put on a Bus: Gets arrested for stealing drugs from the morgue (mostly due to her character being seen as aReplacement Scrappy).

characters.name: Jesse Cardoza
characters.description: Fair Cop: Very easy on the eyes. Killed Off for Real:He dies during Bob Starling’s gas attack on the lab in the Season 9 premiere. We Hardly Knew Ye:Only appears for a single season.

characters.name: Megan Donner
characters.description: Advertised Extra: She featured prominently in ads and was supposed to co-star as a protagonist along with Caine, but the character getsPut on a Busafter 10 episodes in season 1 due to Kim Delaney allegedly not having any chemistry with David Caruso. The Mourning After: Her detective husband died on duty and she never quite got over his death. The series starts with her coming back after a six-month leave, but she quits 10 episodes later, telling Caine working there reminds her too much of her dead husband.

characters.name: Walter Simmons
characters.description: Big Fun: He's a chubby guy with a friendly personality and a sense of humor.

characters.name: Tom Loman
characters.description: The Coroner: The team's Medical Examiner for seasons 8 to 10. Fake Guest Star: Despite being the new ME and Alexx and Tara's permanent replacement, a role that's usually given main credit casting, he's only ever cast as a guest star. I Know Madden Kombat: One episode reveals he's an ace tennis player, and the knowledge is used in helping to solve a case. Ugly Guy, Hot Wife: When he shows the picture of his girlfriend to the team in the finale, everyone is impressed he managed to nail a "model beauty".

characters.name: Samantha Owens
characters.description: Action Girl: She's introduced as a lab tech, but was an ace marksman in the academy which earns her a promotion as a detective where she can work in the field. Accidental Murder:She ends up killing her boyfriend Josh byhitting him with a bottlewhilehe's attacking her.But she fails to be aSympathetic Murdererwhen she leaves an injured Ryan behind to take the blame. Affectionate Nickname: Calls Walter "Teddy Bear". Badass in Distress: She's kidnapped bythe drug dealer working for Josh and Vogel in the last episode, whouses her as a getaway driver at knifepoint. But she manages to escape the situation mostly by herself by driving off a bridge, as the ensuing crash kills the dealer, but leaves her wounded. Chronic Evidence Retention Syndrome:She's exposed as Josh's killer in the end because Ryan finds a piece of glass stuck to her boots after the car crash. Meaning she has been wearing the same boots she had the night of the crime for several days and didn't even think to clean them up.

characters.name: Sam Belmontes
characters.description: The Lab Rat: He's a Chemical Analysis lab tech for seasons 1 and 2.

characters.name: Tyler Jenson
characters.description: The Lab Rat: He's the Audio/visual and multimedia lab technician for seasons 1 to 3.

characters.name: Cynthia Wells
characters.description: The Lab Rat: She is a Questioned Documents Lab Tech from season 2 to 5.

characters.name: Carrie Delgado
characters.description: The Lab Rat: She is a blood evidence technician in season 2. She was a fan of "Quincy, M.E." as a child, influencing her to become a criminalist. We Hardly Knew Ye: She only appears in season 2 due toDeHuffdying from pneumonia.

characters.name: Joseph Kayle
characters.description: The Lab Rat: A lab tech who works in Fingerprints in seasons 2 to 4.

characters.name: Aaron Peters
characters.description: The Lab Rat: He's a Trace Evidence lab tech for seasons 3 and 4.

characters.name: Nicole Talcott
characters.description: The Lab Rat: A DNA lab tech that temporarily fills in for Valera in season 3 after she is suspended.

characters.name: Daniel Cooper
characters.description: The Lab Rat: He's the Audio/Visual and multimedia lab technician for seasons 4 to 6, replacing Tyler. The Resenter: After being forced to resign by Calleigh, he begins to hold a personal grudge against her, and ends up leaking personal information about her online, which results in her being kidnapped by criminals, something he didn't intend.Feeling guilty, he turns himself in in order to give CSI info to help catch the criminals. Resigned in Disgrace: During season 6, he is discovered to have stolen and used a credit card that belonged to the late Tim Speedle, much to the outrage of Eric and Calleigh, and Dan is forced to resign from the lab in disgrace.

characters.name: Jim Markham
characters.description: The Lab Rat: A Ballistic lab tech for season 4.

characters.name: Samantha Barrish
characters.description: Brainy Brunette: An intelligent lab tech with long dark hair. Head-Turning Beauty: Upon meeting her, Ryan immediately takes notice of how attractive she is. The Lab Rat: She is a lab tech who becomes the temporary head of the Audio/Visual Lab after Dan's resignation in season 6. We Hardly Knew Ye: Only appears in 3 episodes in season 6.

characters.name: Dave Benton
characters.description: The Lab Rat: He's the Audio/visual and multimedia lab technician for seasons 7 to 10, replacing Dan.

characters.name: Michael Travers
characters.description: The Lab Rat: A British Trace Analyst that works in CSI from season 7 to 10. I Know Madden Kombat: He has background knowledge about horse racing which is helpful in solving a case.

characters.name: Nikki Vega
characters.description: The Lab Rat: A lab tech who moves to Miami from another crime lab in season 9. We Hardly Knew Ye: Only appears in 2 episodes.

characters.name: Yelina Salas
characters.description: What Happened to the Mouse?: Her final appearance is in the Season 7 finale, when she assists Horatio with an escaped and dying fugitive. Although she was advertised as being billed for more guest appearances, her character and her son's character were dropped with no announcement instead, invokingChuck Cunningham Syndrome.

characters.name: Raymond Caine
characters.description: Dirty Cop: He becomes this after getting hooked on drugs while undercover. Faking the Dead:His death is faked so he can go even deeper undercover. Killed Off for Real:Beaten to death in “Rio”.

characters.name: Rick Stetler
characters.description: A Dick in Name: Richard Stetler is one of the most recurringJerkassesin the cast and the few times Caine called him "Rick" instead of "Stetler" weredefinitely meaning a different diminutive for "Richard". Inspector Javert: Most if not all of his appearances involve him barging into whatever plot is happening in a constant search for Caine's team (and especially Caine) being either criminally incompetent orjust plain criminal, up to and including forcing members of Caine's team to try to play mole. It is unsubtly implied that he keeps doing this because of his beef with Horatio over not getting promoted to Lieutenant. Internal Affairs: He's one of the MDPD's Internal Affairs officers, something he uses to constantly barge into the crime lab looking for corruption. Put on a Bus to Hell: In his final appearance, it is disclosed that for all of him trying to sniff out Caine as aDirty Cop,he is one himself, stealing vehicles impounded by the MDPD to sell off and having a District Attorney assassinated to keep it under wraps. He is arrested and never seen again. The Resenter: Horatio made Lieutenant faster than Stetler. Stetler decides to believe that Caine accomplished this via corruption.

characters.name: Darren Vogel
characters.description: Amoral Attorney: He is not only very good at helping his clients but also threatening his clients to help his other clients. He's also not above framing CSI for tampering with evidence in an attempt to close down the lab. Asshole Victim: He's kidnappedalong with Samand put into aHostage Situationby one of his dissatisfied drug dealer clients and ends up heavily injured whenSam crashes the car. Blackmail: He's an attorney that blackmails hisown clientsafter he helps them hide all their dirty secrets. Back for the Finale: He's the antagonist for one episode in season 8, building up a rivalry with CSI. He comes back for the last two episodes to be the final antagonist of the series. Final Boss: He's the final antagonist of the series in the last two episodes, by hashing a plot to have the lab closed.

characters.name: Josh Avery
characters.description: Cool Boat: Sam mentions he inherited a fancy boat that he didn't like talking about with her. Ryan finds it suspicious a man would never take his girlfriend on a ride on his fancy boat and has it investigated. It turns outHe's using it to smuggle narcotics. Love-Interest Traitor: Uses his connection to Samto gain access to the lab to implant false evidence on Vogel's orders. The Mole:He is actually working for Vogel, and sabotages Sam's work in Vogel's behalf. Romantic False Lead: He's introduced as Sam's boyfriend just as she and Ryan start getting close, and he seems to be better than Ryan in every way.Naturallyhe turns out to be in Vogel's pocket, and frames Sam for tampering with evidence to discredit the lab and free Vogel's client. Victim of the Week: Ends up being the final victim of the show when he turns up dead in the series finale.

characters.name: Marisol Delko
characters.description: Beauty Is Never Tarnished: She's aHead-Turning Beautyand her good looks aren't' affected in the slightest by the fact she's had six months of chemotherapy. Delicate and Sickly: She is diagnosed with cancer and because of the painful treatments, is left weakened enough that her brother Eric has to start helping her to eat. Eventually, he resorts to bringing her marijuana to alleviate her pain and nausea, but that gets them both in heaps of trouble with the FBI, CSI and drug dealers, and she ironically ends up being killed by a hitman sent by the drug dealers. Early-Bird Cameo: Is mentioned by her brother Eric back in season 1, but only makes an actual appearance in season 4. Killed Off for Real: She's killed by a hitmanon the day of her wedding. Love Interest: For Caine in season 4, although most of their relationship occurs offscreen.

characters.name: Kyle Harmon
characters.description: Forced into Evil: He gets blackmailed by Rick Bates to kidnap Kathleen Newberry. Good-Looking Privates: He joins the Army in season eight, and he looks quite fine in the uniform. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Blond and aNice Guy. Important Haircut: He gets one in his second episode in order to look more mature and responsible while testifying in court. Like Father, Unlike Son: He is emotional and impatient, while Horatio isThe Stoicand almost unnaturally patient.
